# Psalms 9

## Verses (20)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | I will give you praise, O Lord, with all my heart; I will make clear all the wonder of your works. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | I will be glad and have delight in you: I will make a song of praise to your name, O Most High. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | When my haters are turned back, they will be broken and overcome before you. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | For you gave approval to my right and my cause; you were seated in your high place judging in righteousness. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | You have said sharp words to the nations, you have sent destruction on the sinners, you have put an end to their name for ever and ever. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | You have given their towns to destruction; the memory of them has gone; they have become waste for ever. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | But the Lord is King for ever: he has made ready his high seat for judging. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | And he will be the judge of the world in righteousness, giving true decisions for the peoples. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | The Lord will be a high tower for those who are crushed down, a high tower in times of trouble; | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | And those who have knowledge of your name will put their faith in you; because you, Lord, have ever given your help to those who were waiting for you. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Make songs of praise to the Lord, whose house is in Zion: make his doings clear to the people. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | When he makes search for blood, he has them in his memory: he is not without thought for the cry of the poor. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | Have mercy on me, O Lord, and see how I am troubled by my haters; let me be lifted up from the doors of death; | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | So that I may make clear all your praise in the house of the daughter of Zion: I will be glad because of your salvation. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | The nations have gone down into the hole which they made: in their secret net is their foot taken. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | The Lord has given knowledge of himself through his judging: the evil-doer is taken in the net which his hands had made. (Higgaion. Selah.) | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | The sinners and all the nations who have no memory of God will be turned into the underworld. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | For the poor will not be without help; the hopes of those in need will not be crushed for ever. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Up! O Lord; let not man overcome you: let the nations be judged before you. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Put them in fear, O Lord, so that the nations may see that they are only men. (Selah.) | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
